items.title: Powers of the Imaginary Unit i
items.summary: The video defines the imaginary unit i as the square root of -1. It then calculates the first several integer powers of i, revealing a repeating cycle of four values.

items.title: Complex Conjugates
items.summary: The video defines the real and imaginary parts of a complex number, then introduces the concept of the complex conjugate. It demonstrates how to represent the conjugate on an Argand diagram and uses it to perform operations like addition and division of complex numbers.

items.title: Introduction to Complex Numbers
items.summary: This video defines complex numbers as numbers with a real and an imaginary part. It shows how to visualize them on the complex plane and perform basic arithmetic like addition, subtraction, and multiplication.

items.title: Regions in the Complex Plane
items.summary: This video demonstrates how to represent inequalities involving complex numbers on the Argand diagram. It illustrates how to find the locus of a complex number by shading the region that satisfies conditions on its real and imaginary parts.

items.title: Locus of Complex Numbers
items.summary: The video explains how to find and sketch the locus of a complex number based on equations involving its modulus. It shows that the locus can be a circle or a line, depending on the form of the equation.

items.title: Using the Second Derivative Test
items.summary: The video explains how to use the second derivative test to find relative extrema of a function. It demonstrates how the sign of the second derivative at a critical point determines if it is a local maximum or minimum.

items.title: Lagrange Multipliers
items.summary: This video introduces the concept of constrained optimization, which involves finding the maximum or minimum of a function subject to a restriction. It explains the method of Lagrange multipliers, where the gradient vectors of the function and the constraint are parallel.

Lagrange Multipliers

items.title: Finding Local Extrema of a Multivariable Function
items.summary: This video explains the process of finding local extrema for a function of two variables. It demonstrates how to locate critical points by setting partial derivatives to zero and then classifies them using the second derivative test.

items.title: Introduction to Constrained Optimization
items.summary: The video introduces constrained optimization, which involves finding the maximum or minimum of a function within a specific set of allowed inputs. It visualizes this concept by overlaying the constraint curve on the function's contour map, showing that the optimum occurs at a point of tangency.

items.title: Introduction to Kuhn-Tucker Conditions
items.summary: This video explains the intuition behind Kuhn-Tucker conditions for constrained optimization problems. It uses a simple example of a hiker on a mountain to illustrate corner solutions and interior solutions.

items.title: Derivative using Chain and Product Rules
items.summary: This video demonstrates how to find the derivative of a function that is a product raised to a power. The speaker shows two different methods for solving the problem, one starting with the chain rule and the other with the product rule, to show they both lead to the same result.

items.summary: This video demonstrates how to differentiate the function y = cos(ax) using the chain rule. It derives the general formula and applies it to an example that also requires the product rule.

items.summary: The video defines partial differentiation and explains the core principle of treating other variables as constants. It then demonstrates how to calculate partial derivatives for a multivariable function using the chain rule.

items.title: Derivatives of Common Functions
items.summary: This video presents the derivative formulas for several common functions without providing proofs. It covers the derivatives of trigonometric functions (sine, cosine, tangent), the exponential function e^x, and the natural logarithm ln(x).

items.title: Applying the Quotient Rule
